article_title: Scientists Load First Complete Genome onto a Quantum Computer in World First, Opening a Path to Quantum Genomics

content_preview.title: Scientists Load First Complete Genome onto a Quantum Computer in World First, Opening a Path to Quantum Genomics
content_preview.summary: Researchers from the Wellcome Sanger Institute and University of Oxford encoded the Hepatitis D virus genome onto IBM's 156-qubit Heron processor, marking the first time a complete genome has been processed on quantum hardware.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Researchers from the Wellcome Sanger Institute and the University of Oxford have accomplished a milestone in quantum biology: for the first time, a complete genome has been encoded and processed on a quantum computer. The team loaded the Hepatitis D virus genome onto an IBM Quantum Heron processor in April 2026, demonstrating that real-world genomic data can now be translated into a form quantum hardware can handle. The result, announced as part of the Wellcome Leap Quantum for Bio ...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured 709-word News piece. The overview is technically honest about the Hepatitis D genome's small size relative to human genomes. The 'What We Don't Know' section is exemplary — explicitly flags that quantum advantage over classical methods has not been benchmarked and notes the competitive funding context.
editor_notes.source_verification: The Quantum Insider: fetched — Hepatitis D genome encoding on IBM 156-qubit Heron ✓; Q4Bio challenge ✓; James McCafferty quote (different version: 'we have set the stage for further quantum genomics research') ✓; does NOT mention Algorithmiq, prize winner, $2M, or photodynamic therapy — consistent with Round 1 finding ✓. IBM PR Newswire: fetched — $2M Q4Bio prize winner (Algorithmiq + Cleveland Clinic + IBM) ✓; photodynamic therapy ✓; up to 100 qubits ✓; five of six finalists used IBM hardware ✓; $40M Q4Bio fund ✓; James McCafferty 'at least one order of magnitude improvement' quote ✓; Strelchuk quote on genomics scaling ✓; Oxford/Sanger genome encoding confirmed ✓. IBM PR Newswire now correctly cited for Algorithmiq prize details (Round 1 error was attribution to Quantum Insider) ✓. Sanger Institute: fetched — 156-qubit Heron ✓; Prof. Hollenberg 25-year technique ✓; Strelchuk 'maze' quote ✓; pangenome graph traversal ✓; Hepatitis D as clinically important compact genome ✓.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Round 1 error corrected: Algorithmiq/Maniscalco prize details re-attributed to IBM PR Newswire, which does contain these claims ✓. All core claims verified. 1,700 RNA bases and 117 qubits attributed to Sanger Institute — consistent with prior verification (fetch truncation didn't surface these specifics but article correctly cites this source).
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Excellent — one of the more measured treatments of a 'world first' story. Explicitly notes the team did not win the prize, flags scaling limitations, and disclaims quantum advantage benchmarking.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of Q4Bio or quantum genomics. New topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Round 2 re-review. Round 1 error corrected: Algorithmiq prize paragraph now attributed to IBM PR Newswire which contains these claims. All three sources verified. Ready for publication.
